senses.definition: an instance or stage before a shipment occurs
examples: The preship inspection verified the package contents.
We conducted a preship check to ensure all items were packed correctly.

senses.definition: the period before a shipment is sent, or the act of preparing for shipment
examples: We updated the preshipment documents.
Preshipment checks are required by the carrier.

senses.definition: already prepared or sent before shipment would normally occur
examples: The preshipped orders were ready for dispatch.
Preshipped items were logged correctly.

senses.definition: the process of preparing for shipment; the act of shipping in advance
examples: Preshipping analysis helped reduce delays.
Preshipping activities include labeling and packaging.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of preship; to ship something before the usual shipment time
examples: The company preships small orders to test market response.
They preship items when demand spikes.

senses.definition: an anticipated or planned temporary shortage in inventory before restocking
examples: We faced a preshortage of parts before the next delivery.
Preshortage was expected due to supplier delays.

senses.definition: to shorten something in advance or beforehand
examples: They preshorten the project timeline to meet the deadline.
We preshorten the list to focus on priorities.

senses.definition: an event or sequence of previews before the main show
examples: The theater offered a preshow performance.
There was a short preshow to introduce the film.

senses.definition: to show previews or introductions before the main program
examples: The streaming service preshowed trailers before the movie.
They preshow the upcoming episodes.

senses.definition: shown or presented as a preshow in the past
examples: Preshowed clips were played before the feature.
The preshowged trailers were well received.

senses.definition: the act of presenting previews before a main event
examples: Preshowing is common at film festivals.
The preshowing of trailers lasts several minutes.

senses.definition: displayed as a preshow before the main event
examples: Preshown clips were circulated online.
The preshown footage drew attention.

senses.definition: plural of preshow; previews before the main event
examples: Preshows included interviews and trailers.
Audience members arrived during preshows.

senses.definition: past tense of preshrink; to shrink something in advance
examples: The fabric preshrank to fit the measurements.
We preshrank the sleeves before tailoring.

senses.definition: to shrink something before use or sale
examples: Preshrink the towels to prevent later shrinkage.
We preshrink fabrics to ensure a good fit.

senses.definition: the act or rate of shrinking that occurs before use
examples: Preshrinkage helps reduce later distortion.
We measured preshrinkage to select the right size.

senses.definition: the process of shrinking fabric before use
examples: Preshrinking improves fabric stability.
Preshrinking is often recommended for cotton.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of preshrink; to shrink fabric beforehand
examples: The label preshrinks fabrics to ensure size accuracy.
They preshrinks all cotton garments.

senses.definition: fabric or material that has already shrunk before sale or use
examples: Preshrunk cotton T-shirts stay true to size.
Preshrunk denim reduces surprise shrinkage.

senses.definition: fabric that has previously shrunk; already shrunk
examples: Preshrunken fabric is ready for sewing.
The preshrunken denim fits the pattern well.

senses.definition: to be in charge of a meeting, ceremony, or organization and lead it
examples: The chair will preside over the conference.
She presided at the annual awards ceremony.

senses.definition: past tense of preside
examples: She presided over the meeting yesterday.
The judge presided at the trial.

senses.definition: the office or role of president; the period of being president
examples: During his presidence, the country saw reforms.
The presidence of the current chair ends soon.

senses.definition: Spanish term for the presidency; the office of the president
examples: La presidencia de México es un cargo clave.
Durante su presidencia, se aprobaron reformas.

senses.definition: the terms during which persons hold the office of president
examples: There have been several presidencies since independence.
Different presidencies brought different policies.

senses.definition: the office or position of a president; the period during which one serves as president
examples: She will run for the presidency next year.
The presidency has faced many challenges.
